data.title: Crypto Cracks Late in Day, Bitcoin Slumps Below $106K
data.description:
• A modestly down day in cryptocurrencies morphed into a deeper selloff late in the in the U.S. day on Thursday.
  • Above $108,000 a few hours ago, bitcoin slid below $106,000.
  • Trump tariff threats and boosted Middle East tensions were among the headlines, though other risk assets didn't seem affected.

data.title: Coinbase to Launch Bitcoin Rewards Card With Amex, While Eyeing U.S. Futures Expansion
data.description:
• Coinbase plans to launch a credit card offering 4% bitcoin cashback in partnership with American Express.
  • The move aligns with a trend of crypto exchanges introducing payment cards to spend digital asset holdings for everyday purchases.
  • The announcement coincides with Coinbase's potential plans to bring perpetual futures trading to the U.S.

data.title: Shopify to Enable USDC Payments on Coinbase's Base for Merchants Worldwide
data.description:
• USDC stablecoin payments will be made available on Base to millions of Shopify merchants worldwide.
  • The initiative is designed to streamline global commerce with crypto-native infrastructure.

data.title: Bitcoin Will Rally as U.S. Growth Improves, Crypto Bills Progress: Coinbase Research
data.description:
• A positive macroeconomic outlook and increased regulatory clarity are expected to boost crypto markets in the second half of 2025, according to Coinbase Research.
  • Public companies are increasingly adding crypto to their balance sheets, though this trend introduces new systemic risks.
  • The GENIUS Act and CLARITY Act could reshape the regulatory landscape for digital assets, with significant implications for issuers and investors.

data.title: DeFi Adding $5B of Solana Buying Power With New Line of Credit
data.description:
• DeFi Development has secured a $5 billion equity line of credit with RK Capital Management to boost its SOL holdings.
  • The company is part of the growing trend of publicly-traded firms pivoting to a crypto treasury strategy, accumulating tokens by selling shares and debt.
  • DFDV shares were up 12% in Thursday U.S. trade.

data.title: NEAR Protocol Plunges 9% as Volatility Spikes Despite User Growth
data.description:
• NEAR Protocol experienced an 8.6% decline, dropping from $2.696 to $2.492 with significant sell-off volume spikes.
  • Despite price struggles, NEAR has become the second most used layer-1 blockchain with 46 million monthly active users in May 2025, trailing only Solana.
  • DWF Labs has challenged NEAR Protocol to reduce token inflation from 5% to 2.5%, offering to purchase an additional 10 million tokens if implemented.

data.title: AVAX Rebounds From Key Support After 6% Plunge
data.description:
• Avalanche's AVAX token found support after a significant short-term decline, according to CoinDesk Research.
  • AVAX underperformed the CoinDesk 20 index, dropping 5.6% compared to the index's 3.2% loss.
  • A high-volume support zone between $20.76 and $20.85 helped reverse the downtrend with strong buying interest.

data.title: TON Slips as Selling Pressure Mounts Despite Recovery Attempts
data.description:
• Telegram's cryptocurrency, TON, experienced a 4.67% intraday decline on high volume, indicating strong selling pressure.
  • The token is down 3% in the last 24 hours, with new resistance forming at $3.24.
  • A recovery pattern was observed, with prices climbing from $3.19 to $3.20, representing a 0.4% gain.

data.title: Singapore's Trident Digital Targets Mammoth $500M Raise to Establish XRP Treasury
data.description:
• Trident Digital aims to raise $500 million to create a corporate treasury focused on XRP.
  • The initiative will involve long-term XRP holdings and partnerships within Ripple's ecosystem.
  • The treasury launch is planned for 2025, with Chaince Securities advising the project.

data.title: Crypto Lending Platform Morpho V2 Brings DeFi Closer to Traditional Finance
data.description:
• Morpho V2 allows users to make offers instead of pre-allocating capital with market-driven pricing to set interest rates, rather than rigid formulas.
  • Whereas the previous version of Morpho limited loans to single collateral types, V2 supports single assets, multiple assets, or entire portfolios as collateral, including RWAs and niche assets.
  • V2 will be rolled out in the coming weeks.
